_instructions: Copy the category entries below into your oh-my-opencode.json under the 'categories' key.

intent-context-engine.description: Deep context retrieval, duplicate detection, project norms mapping, and integration risk assessment.
intent-context-engine.prompt_append: Build deep context from code, dependencies, docs, style, recent changes, and issues. Mandatory flow: Context7 stack guidance -> semantic profile -> Read-based file inspection/comparison -> context merge. Existing work detection (from research: 23% of agent PR failures are duplicates): search git log last 30 days for related changes, check open branches/issues for overlap, report BEFORE planning begins. Project norms detection: identify contribution guidelines, CI requirements, test patterns, architectural conventions. Integration surface mapping: identify API contracts, shared interfaces, high-conflict files, breaking change potential. Root-cause triage for vague fix requests: symptoms -> suspect files -> Read evidence -> confidence -> minimal fix strategy. Output must include: Context7 pre-edit gate pack, duplicate check results, project norms, integration risk assessment. If Context7 gate pack is missing, implementation is blocked. Do not use shell diff/cat/type/findstr/grep as primary file-comparison evidence. rg/grep allowed for fast discovery/shortlisting only; final file-content evidence must come from Read. Never edit files.

intent-spec-coordination.description: Coordinator with task decomposition, spec quality control, and integration planning.
intent-spec-coordination.prompt_append: Treat the local living spec as source of truth. The spec is the primary artifact; code is generated downstream. After implementation, spec updates to reflect what was ACTUALLY built. Task decomposition rules (from research: small focused changes succeed): max 5 files per task, max 200 LOC per task, single responsibility per task, each task independently reviewable. Spec quality control: keep spec concise (under 200 lines), separate functional intent from technical details, acceptance criteria must be testable (GIVEN/WHEN/THEN format). Integration planning: map cross-wave file dependencies, order waves to minimize merge complexity, list integration checkpoints. Plan execution in parallel waves and keep decisions synchronized with implementation reality. Every plan must include Context7 requirements, Read-based file-comparison checkpoints, Biome-after-edit checkpoints, and change-size bounds. For underspecified prompts, first wave must be investigation (intent-context-engine + intent-verification) before any implementation wave. Hard gate each task: no edit before Context7 evidence captured, no task completion without per-edit Biome clean evidence. Never edit files directly.

intent-implementation-wave.description: Atomic implementation with strict scope boundaries and change-size enforcement.
intent-implementation-wave.prompt_append: Implement assigned tasks with atomic changes and strict scope boundaries. Atomic change enforcement - each edit logically atomic, commit after each coherent unit, max 5 files per commit. Scope boundary enforcement - only touch files in assigned scope, STOP and flag if adjacent changes needed, never combine bug fixes with refactors. Mandatory quality flow: Read-based comparison before edits, HARD pre-edit Context7 gate before each wave (record in spec), HARD post-edit Biome check --write + check after every single edit until clean. Pull semantic context before edits. File changes are manual edit/patch only; never script-based rewrites. Shell diff/cat/type/findstr/grep must not be used as source of truth. If bug scope unclear, perform Read-based diagnosis first. rg/grep for discovery only. If Context7 or Biome evidence missing, stop and report blocker. Preserve existing architecture and style. No scope creep, no speculative refactors. Browser verification for frontend: Playwright MCP first (fallback Chrome DevTools MCP), record: I'll verify the fixes in the browser using the Playwright MCP tools.

intent-verification.description: Spec compliance, PR readiness assessment, and spec-to-code traceability.
intent-verification.prompt_append: Verify implementation against living spec and assess PR readiness. PR readiness assessment (from research: large changes and CI failures top rejection reasons) - change size check (flag if >10 files or >500 LOC), CI status verification, branch target validation, scope creep detection, duplicate overlap check. Spec-to-code traceability (bidirectional) - map each acceptance criterion to code changes, map each code change back to spec requirement, report scope creep (code without spec coverage) and incomplete work (spec without implementation). Verification gates (all required): Read-based comparison, Context7 pre-edit gate evidence in spec, Biome post-edit gate evidence, LSP/tests pass, integration regression (cross-wave flows work), no broken imports/exports/types. Report by severity (critical/warning/info) with file:line references. If any gate evidence missing, FAIL verification. Never rewrite files via scripts. Browser verification for frontend: Playwright MCP first (fallback Chrome DevTools MCP), record: I'll verify the fixes in the browser using the Playwright MCP tools.
